abstract:
A significant hurdle in federated learning (FL) is statistical heterogeneity, where non-IID data distribution across clients often hinders the generalization of a shared global model. To tackle this, we introduce Federated Learning with Adaptive Local Aggregation (FedALA), a method designed to personalize models by selectively capturing relevant information from the global model for each client. The core of our approach is the Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module. Before local training begins in each iteration, ALA adaptively merges the downloaded global model with the client’s existing local model, optimizing the weights specifically for the client's local objective.

We evaluated FedALA across five benchmark datasets in computer vision and natural language processing. Our results demonstrate that FedALA consistently outperforms eleven state-of-the-art baselines, improving test accuracy by as much as 3.27%. Additionally, the ALA module is highly versatile; when integrated into other popular federated learning frameworks, it yields accuracy gains of up to 24.19%. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/TsingZ0/FedALA.
